The app that never asked you to install it and yet you use it every single day. That’s the quiet power of PWAs in 2026.
The hot take back in about 2016, when PWAs first became an actual thing: “They will kill native apps. Close to a decade later, native apps are still here, the App Store is going strong and PWAs have clearly not taken over the world. But here is what nobody wants to say out loud; they didn’t die either. Actually, in 2026 they are even more immediate — just not in the way any of that hype had promised.
Let’s cut through the noise.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

What the PWAs Actually are (and why we broadened the definition)
A Progressive Web App (PWA) is a web application that leverages best practices of how you would develop to make it function like a native installed app by leveraging standard HTML, CSS, and Javascript. Three pillars define it:

• Installable — Add to home screen without app store

• Offline Capable — Service Workers cache resources, which enable the core flows to survive dead zones

• Re-engageable — Get Push notifications even when the app is killed
